name: ws09: build-script — fix cmd_ui to point at clawft-ui not ui
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #1 + Open Questions #1 + Orphaned/Stale Work.

## Problem / gap

`scripts/build.sh::cmd_ui()` (lines 195-213, 264-266) checks `[ -d "$ROOT/ui" ] && [ -f "$ROOT/ui/package.json" ]` and runs `cd "$ROOT/ui" && npm run build`. Per CHANGELOG 0.6.19 (2026-04-22) the directory was renamed `ui/` -> `clawft-ui/`. The old path no longer has a `package.json` (only stale 2026-02-27 `dist/` and `node_modules/`), so the cmd silently `skip`s — the UI never builds via `scripts/build.sh ui` or `scripts/build.sh all`. This is a release-gate blocker for the dashboard ship.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `scripts/build.sh::cmd_ui` references `$ROOT/clawft-ui` (not `$ROOT/ui`) for the directory + `package.json` check and the `cd` target.
- [ ] Dry-run print lines (audit cites lines 202 and 407) updated to the new path.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh ui` builds the dashboard end-to-end against `clawft-ui/` and produces `clawft-ui/dist/`.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh all` includes the UI build in its phase chain.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh gate` continues to pass with the new path.

## Dependencies

- None. Pure tooling fix.

## Notes

Pair with the `weft ui --ui-dir` default fix (separate item) so the build output and the runtime serve path agree.

name: ws09: weft-ui — update --ui-dir default to clawft-ui/dist
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #2 + Orphaned/Stale Work.

## Problem / gap

`crates/clawft-cli/src/commands/ui_cmd.rs:122` and `crates/clawft-cli/src/help_text.rs:119-122` still default `--ui-dir` to `./ui/dist`. After the rename `ui/` -> `clawft-ui/` (CHANGELOG 0.6.19) `weft ui` will either serve the stale 2026-02-27 build artefact at `ui/dist/` or fail when that directory is removed. The matching unit test at `ui_cmd.rs:127` asserts `Some("./ui/dist")` and would also need updating.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crates/clawft-cli/src/commands/ui_cmd.rs:122` default changed to `./clawft-ui/dist`.
- [ ] `crates/clawft-cli/src/help_text.rs:119-122` help text references `./clawft-ui/dist`.
- [ ] Unit test at `ui_cmd.rs:127` updated to expect `Some("./clawft-ui/dist")`.
- [ ] `weft ui --no-open` with no `--ui-dir` flag serves a freshly built `clawft-ui/dist/` bundle.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes after the change.

## Dependencies

- Builds best on top of the `scripts/build.sh::cmd_ui` rename fix so the served bundle is also the freshly built one.

## Notes

Land both rename fixes in the same PR if possible to avoid an interim where build script and CLI default disagree.

name: ws09: hygiene — remove stale ui/ legacy artefact
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #3 + Open Questions #2 + Orphaned/Stale Work.

## Problem / gap

`ui/` (top-level repo dir) still exists with a 2026-02-27 `dist/` (459 KB JS) and a `node_modules/` but no `package.json`. It is the pre-rename leftover. With the build script and `weft ui` defaults pointing here it can serve a months-old bundle. Even after the path defaults are fixed, having the stale tree on disk is misleading and bloats checkouts.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decide: delete entirely vs keep as a forwarding alias / symlink to `clawft-ui/`.
- [ ] `ui/dist/` and `ui/node_modules/` removed from the working tree.
- [ ] `.gitignore` updated if the alias approach is chosen so the legacy path cannot accumulate stale artefacts again.
- [ ] Repo grep for `\bui/dist\b` returns no live references (only historical CHANGELOG / planning notes).
- [ ] CI / release scripts do not fail on the missing path.

## Dependencies

- Lands cleanly only after the `scripts/build.sh` and `weft ui` path fixes (otherwise removal breaks them).

## Notes

If retained as a symlink, be careful on Windows checkouts — a plain delete is the lowest-risk option.

name: ws09: package-json — rename name field from ui to clawft-ui
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #4 + Open Questions #3 + Orphaned/Stale Work.

## Problem / gap

`clawft-ui/package.json` still has `"name": "ui"` and `"version": "0.0.0"` — leftover from the original scaffold and the pre-rename state. Cosmetic but inconsistent with workspace conventions and confusing in error messages and lockfile output.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `clawft-ui/package.json` `name` set to `clawft-ui`.
- [ ] `version` aligned with workspace versioning convention (matches the Rust crate / workspace cycle, e.g. `0.7.0`).
- [ ] `package-lock.json` / `pnpm-lock.yaml` regenerated to match.
- [ ] `npm run build` still succeeds.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Bundle this with the README and index.html title hygiene fixes since they all touch top-level `clawft-ui/` files.

name: ws09: index-html — set real product title for dashboard
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #5 + Orphaned/Stale Work.

## Problem / gap

`clawft-ui/index.html` still has `<title>ui</title>` from the Vite scaffold. Browser tab and bookmark UX shows the placeholder.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `<title>` updated to a product-appropriate string (e.g. `Clawft Agent Dashboard`).
- [ ] Optionally surface dynamic title per route via TanStack Router head metadata.
- [ ] Build still succeeds; smoke-load shows the new title.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Trivial; bundle with the package.json rename and README rewrite.

name: ws09: readme — replace Vite template with project README
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #6 + Orphaned/Stale Work.

## Problem / gap

`clawft-ui/README.md` is still the literal Vite template ("React + TypeScript + Vite", links to `@vitejs/plugin-react`, talks about React Compiler not being enabled). No project-specific content for contributors who land in `clawft-ui/` first.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] README rewritten covering: local dev with MSW mocks, Axum-mode dev (against `weft gateway`), browser-mode dev (WASM), build (`scripts/build.sh ui`), deployment (referencing `docs/ui/deployment.md`).
- [ ] Links to `docs/ui/{developer-guide,api-reference,browser-mode,deployment}.md`.
- [ ] Mentions the BackendAdapter mode-selection contract (`?mode=`, `VITE_BACKEND_MODE`, runtime probe).
- [ ] No leftover Vite template boilerplate.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Keep it short — link out to `docs/ui/` for depth.

name: ws09: api — add CSP middleware to gateway handlers
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #7 + TODO at `crates/clawft-services/src/api/handlers.rs:130`.

## Problem / gap

`handlers.rs:130` carries `// TODO: Add Content-Security-Policy (CSP) middleware via tower layer.` Plan-of-record (S3.5) specifies `script-src 'self'`, no inline scripts, restricted resource origins, WebSocket origin allowlist, and `'wasm-unsafe-eval'` only in browser-only mode. Without CSP the dashboard is exposed to XSS escalation paths once it ships behind shared hosting or the agent gateway.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Tower CSP layer mounted on the api router with the policy from the plan-of-record.
- [ ] Browser-only mode toggles `'wasm-unsafe-eval'`; default mode does not include it.
- [ ] WebSocket `connect-src` origin allowlist matches the served origin.
- [ ] Integration test asserts `Content-Security-Policy` header on at least one HTML and one JSON response.
- [ ] TODO comment at `handlers.rs:130` removed.

## Dependencies

- None blocking. Coordinate with rate-limiting middleware item to land them as a single S3.5 hardening PR.

## Notes

If any inline `<script>` or `style` is required, document it in the PR and prefer `nonce`-based allowance over `'unsafe-inline'`.

name: ws09: api — add rate-limiting middleware with per-endpoint defaults
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #8 + TODO at `crates/clawft-services/src/api/handlers.rs:133`.

## Problem / gap

`handlers.rs:133` carries `// TODO: Add rate limiting middleware via tower::limit::RateLimitLayer or tower-governor.` The TODO comment already documents per-endpoint defaults: `/api/auth/token` 5/min, `/api/delegation/*` 60/min, `/api/monitoring/*` 30/min. Without it, the unauthenticated `POST /api/auth/token` is a brute-force surface and the streaming endpoints can be DOS'd.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Rate limiter (tower-governor or equivalent) mounted on the api router.
- [ ] Per-endpoint policies match the documented defaults (auth 5/min, delegation 60/min, monitoring 30/min); other endpoints get a sane global default.
- [ ] 429 response includes `Retry-After` header.
- [ ] Integration test asserts a burst of `POST /api/auth/token` returns 429 after the configured ceiling.
- [ ] TODO comment at `handlers.rs:133` removed.

## Dependencies

- Pairs naturally with CSP middleware in a single S3.5 hardening PR.

## Notes

Prefer in-memory limiter for the local-gateway use case; document upgrade path to a shared store if/when multi-instance comes.

name: ws09: api — add WebSocket heartbeat and dead-connection cleanup
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #9 (S3.5.4 sparc 03 phase doc).

## Problem / gap

The `/ws` endpoint in `crates/clawft-services/src/api/ws.rs` accepts subscribe/unsubscribe/ping but does not enforce a server-side heartbeat or evict dead connections. Long-running dashboards leak slots and the broadcaster fans out to phantom subscribers. S3.5.4 in the sparc plan is unimplemented.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Server sends periodic ping (e.g. 30s) and closes connections that miss N pongs.
- [ ] Client (`clawft-ui/src/lib/ws-client.ts`) responds to server pings and surfaces missed-pong reconnect.
- [ ] Broadcaster removes dropped subscribers from its topic map promptly.
- [ ] Integration / unit test confirms a stalled client is evicted within the configured window.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Reuse `axum::extract::ws` keepalive primitives if available; otherwise a `tokio::time::interval` task per socket is the simplest path.

name: ws09: api-bridge — wire skill install/uninstall to real loader
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #10 + TODOs at `crates/clawft-services/src/api/bridge.rs:282` and `:287`.

## Problem / gap

`bridge.rs:282` (`// TODO: implement skill installation via ClawHub registry`) and `:287` (`// TODO: implement skill uninstallation`) both currently return `Err("not implemented")`. The `/skills` UI route renders an Install/Uninstall button that always fails. Real implementation depends on Skill loader (C3) and ClawHub registry (K4) main-sprint deliverables.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `install_skill(slug)` calls into the real Skill loader and ClawHub registry; success returns the installed skill record.
- [ ] `uninstall_skill(slug)` removes from the loader and from local cache.
- [ ] UI install/uninstall round-trip works end-to-end against a running `weft gateway`.
- [ ] Error responses surface a useful message when a slug is unknown / network fails.
- [ ] TODO markers at `bridge.rs:282` and `:287` removed.

## Dependencies

- C3 Skill loader / hot-reload
- K4 ClawHub registry

## Notes

Until C3/K4 land the UI button should remain feature-gated or grey out with a `not yet available` message.

name: ws09: api-bridge — implement memory delete with append-only rewrite
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #11 + TODO at `crates/clawft-services/src/api/bridge.rs:395`.

## Problem / gap

`bridge.rs:395` carries `// TODO: implement memory entry deletion`. Underlying memory store files are append-only, so a delete requires rewriting the file (or tombstone-marking entries). The `/memory` UI exposes a delete affordance that currently no-ops.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Delete-by-id implemented either via tombstone + compaction or atomic rewrite.
- [ ] HNSW index updated so a deleted entry no longer surfaces in semantic search.
- [ ] Concurrency: delete during writes does not corrupt the file.
- [ ] UI delete round-trip removes the row from `/memory` after refresh.
- [ ] TODO marker at `bridge.rs:395` removed.

## Dependencies

- H2 Memory store HNSW (already partly wired).

## Notes

Prefer tombstone+compact-on-startup for crash safety; document the recovery semantics in `docs/ui/api-reference.md`.

name: ws09: api-bridge — implement save_config persistence
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #12 + TODO at `crates/clawft-services/src/api/bridge.rs:467`.

## Problem / gap

`bridge.rs:467` carries `// TODO: implement config persistence (deserialize, validate, write to file)`. `save_config` returns `Err("config saving not yet implemented")`. The `/config` UI route shows a Save button (and a diff summary) that always errors.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `save_config` deserializes the incoming JSON into the `RoutingConfig` / agent-config types.
- [ ] Validation runs (schema + cross-field invariants) before write.
- [ ] Atomic write (tempfile + rename) to the same file `weft` reads from at startup.
- [ ] API key fields remain write-only / never echoed back (already the GET-side contract).
- [ ] UI Save button persists and a daemon restart picks up the new config.
- [ ] TODO marker at `bridge.rs:467` removed.

## Dependencies

- None blocking; aligns with the recent `cwd-relative workspace config overlay` work.

## Notes

Decide whether save also triggers a hot-reload signal to the running daemon or only takes effect on restart; document the choice.

name: ws09: api — replace mock delegation handlers with FlowDelegator wiring
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #13 + Mock-Data Backends section (`api/delegation.rs::list_active_delegations` line 92, `list_delegation_rules` line 131, `upsert`, `delete`, `history`).

## Problem / gap

All four delegation handlers in `crates/clawft-services/src/api/delegation.rs` return hardcoded fixtures (three mock delegations, mock rules). The `/delegation` UI's Active / Rules / History tabs render that fake data. Real wiring depends on FlowDelegator events from M1/M2.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `list_active_delegations` subscribes to / queries the live FlowDelegator state.
- [ ] `list_delegation_rules` reads from config (already persisted by the routing config layer).
- [ ] `upsert` and `delete` write rule changes through to config persistence.
- [ ] `history` returns persisted delegation events (audit-trail / event-sourced store).
- [ ] `/delegation` UI shows live data when the daemon is running.
- [ ] Comments "Mock data for now" / "Mock rules" removed.

## Dependencies

- M1/M2 FlowDelegator real events.
- Depends on `save_config` persistence work for rule writes.

## Notes

UI is already shaped correctly per audit ("UI ready, backend mocked").

name: ws09: api — replace mock monitoring handlers with metrics collector
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #14 + Mock-Data Backends section (`api/monitoring.rs::token_usage` line 99, `cost_breakdown`, `pipeline_runs`).

## Problem / gap

`crates/clawft-services/src/api/monitoring.rs` returns hardcoded provider/session totals and ADR-026 tier costs. The `/monitoring` UI renders those fakes. Real wiring depends on metrics collector + sender_id thread (D5/D6).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `token_usage`, `cost_breakdown`, `pipeline_runs` query the real metrics collector (live or persisted).
- [ ] Cost attribution by `sender_id` reflects D6 thread once landed.
- [ ] Per-session latency reflects D5.
- [ ] `/monitoring` UI shows non-zero values during a real agent run.
- [ ] "Mock data" comment removed.

## Dependencies

- D5 per-session latency.
- D6 cost attribution by sender_id.

## Notes

If the metrics store is not yet implemented, ship a minimal in-process aggregator first and document the upgrade path to a persisted store.

name: ws09: tools — wire render_ui to message bus and ws broadcaster
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #15 + step4-s2.1-live-canvas.md design note 2.

## Problem / gap

`crates/clawft-tools/src/render_ui.rs` validates and logs Canvas commands but does not publish them to the message bus. The whole point of the Live Canvas (`/canvas` route + `CanvasCommand` protocol) is that an agent calling `render_ui` causes the dashboard to render; today nothing reaches the WS broadcaster.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `render_ui` publishes the validated command to the message bus on success.
- [ ] WS broadcaster fans the command out on the `canvas` topic.
- [ ] `/canvas` route receives and renders the command in real time.
- [ ] End-to-end test: agent invokes `render_ui` -> dashboard tab subscribed to `canvas` shows the new element.
- [ ] Existing validation and logging behaviour preserved.

## Dependencies

- WebSocket heartbeat work makes the demo experience cleaner but is not strictly blocking.

## Notes

This is the single highest-impact gap for the Canvas feature — without it the route is decorative.

name: ws09: wasm-adapter — implement getToolSchema introspection
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #16 + TODO at `clawft-ui/src/lib/adapters/wasm-adapter.ts:205`.

## Problem / gap

`wasm-adapter.ts:205` carries `// Schema introspection deferred to future WASM entry point` and `getToolSchema()` returns `null` in WASM mode. Browser-mode users on `/tools` see no JSON-Schema viewer for any tool.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `clawft-wasm` exposes a `tool_schema(slug)` entry point returning JSON-Schema for the registered tool.
- [ ] `WasmAdapter.getToolSchema()` calls it and returns the schema.
- [ ] `/tools` route shows JSON-Schema in WASM mode equivalently to Axum mode.
- [ ] Comment at `wasm-adapter.ts:205` removed.

## Dependencies

- W-BROWSER WASM bindgen surface (audit lists this as cross-workstream open dep).

## Notes

Keep schema generation symmetric with the Axum-side handler so both modes return the same shape.

name: ws09: ui — implement real Cmd+K command palette
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #17 + Open Questions #7 (S1.3.12).

## Problem / gap

Per step3-s1.3-core-views.md the Cmd+K shortcut is a placeholder that only opens / closes a stub. SPARC plan called for a real fuzzy-finder palette over routes, commands, agents, sessions, etc.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Cmd+K opens a fuzzy-search palette indexing routes, agents, sessions, tools, skills, channels.
- [ ] Keyboard-only navigation (arrow keys, enter, escape).
- [ ] Recent / pinned items surfaced first.
- [ ] Accessible (focus trap, ARIA roles, visible focus ring).
- [ ] Works in both Axum and WASM adapter modes.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Land alongside the WCAG audit so a11y patterns are consistent.

name: ws09: auth — add use-auth hook with single-use URL token
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #18 + Open Questions #6 (S1.2.8).

## Problem / gap

No `use-auth.ts` hook exists. Token handling is inlined in `lib/api-client.ts` against `localStorage["clawft-token"]`. The SPARC plan called for one-time URL-token consumption (single-use, consumed on first read) — currently the token from `weft ui`'s `?token=...` URL is read freely on every load.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `clawft-ui/src/lib/use-auth.ts` (or `hooks/use-auth.ts`) added with token lifecycle helpers.
- [ ] URL `?token=...` is consumed once: read on first load, persisted to localStorage, then stripped from `window.location` via `history.replaceState`.
- [ ] `api-client.ts` reads the token via the hook (or shared store), not directly from localStorage.
- [ ] Logout clears localStorage and refuses to silently re-auth from a now-stale URL.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/ui/api-reference.md`.

## Dependencies

- Pairs naturally with Tailscale auth provider work for the multi-user story.

## Notes

Keep the 24h TTL behaviour of `POST /api/auth/token` aligned with the hook's refresh policy.

name: ws09: browser-config — validate cors_proxy URL is HTTPS in production
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #19 + Open Questions #9.

## Problem / gap

`clawft-ui/src/components/wasm/browser-config.tsx` accepts a `cors_proxy` URL for routing OpenAI / custom provider traffic in browser-only mode. S3.6 security req specifies HTTPS in production, HTTP only for localhost. The check is not visibly enforced in the component.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Save action rejects `http://` URLs unless host resolves to localhost / 127.0.0.1 / `::1`.
- [ ] User-visible error explains why HTTP was rejected.
- [ ] Validation runs on initial provider-config load too (not just on user input) so legacy stored values are flagged.
- [ ] Unit test covers the validator with HTTPS, HTTP-localhost, and HTTP-public cases.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Keep the validator pure so it can be reused in future Tauri shell config screens.

name: ws09: pwa — add manifest, service worker, push notifications
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #20 + Deferred Phases S3.3.

## Problem / gap

No `manifest.webmanifest`, no `service-worker.*`, no offline shell, no WASM binary cache, no SW + WS push-notification bridge. S3.3 is entirely unstarted. Long-horizon polish, not 0.7 critical.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `manifest.webmanifest` with name, icons, theme color, start_url, display=standalone.
- [ ] Service worker pre-caches the app shell and the WASM binary.
- [ ] Offline reload renders the shell with a clear offline banner.
- [ ] Push notifications wired to a WS event bridge (subscribe via VAPID, dispatch via SW).
- [ ] Lighthouse PWA score > 90 in CI.

## Dependencies

- None blocking.

## Notes

Deferred per phase plan; bundle with mobile responsive work.

name: ws09: responsive — mobile sidebar drawer and chat input
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #21 + Deferred Phases S3.3.

## Problem / gap

`MainLayout.tsx` has no responsive drawer for screens < 768 px. Mobile WebChat lacks a bottom-anchored input and swipe nav between sessions. S3.3 is entirely unstarted.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Sidebar collapses to a drawer below 768 px with hamburger toggle.
- [ ] `/chat` mobile layout: bottom-anchored input, message list scroll-up, swipe-left/right to switch sessions.
- [ ] Tap targets meet WCAG 44x44 minimum.
- [ ] Existing desktop layout unaffected (visual snapshot baseline holds at >= 1024 px).

## Dependencies

- WCAG audit (separate item).

## Notes

Keep Tailwind v4 breakpoint conventions consistent across the app.

name: ws09: tauri — scaffold clawft-ui/src-tauri desktop shell
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #22 + Deferred Phases S3.4 + ADR-038 cross-reference.

## Problem / gap

No `clawft-ui/src-tauri/` directory. The agent dashboard's Tauri shell is unstarted and distinct from `gui/src-tauri/` (which is the WeftOS Explorer). Plan calls for: Tauri 2.0 wrapping `clawft-ui/dist/`, system tray with agent-status colour, Cmd+Shift+W global hotkey, auto-start `weft gateway`, macOS Spotlight integration, native notification bridge.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `clawft-ui/src-tauri/` initialized for Tauri 2.0 wrapping `clawft-ui/dist/`.
- [ ] System tray icon with agent-status colour states.
- [ ] Global hotkey Cmd+Shift+W (Linux/Win equivalents).
- [ ] Tauri side-car launches `weft gateway` on app start, terminates on quit.
- [ ] macOS Spotlight registration documented; native notification bridge functional on Linux/Win/macOS.
- [ ] Build artefact ships as part of `scripts/build.sh all`.

## Dependencies

- None blocking; reuse `gui/src-tauri/` learnings without sharing the package.

## Notes

Deferred behind 0.9.x UX redesign cycle per audit.

name: ws09: tests — add Playwright E2E suite under clawft-ui/tests
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #23 + Deferred Phases S3.5.

## Problem / gap

No `clawft-ui/tests/` directory. `package.json` has no `playwright` / `vitest` / `cypress` dep. The dashboard ships without an automated browser-level smoke suite.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Playwright installed in `clawft-ui/package.json` with deterministic version pin.
- [ ] `clawft-ui/tests/` covers: dashboard loads, WebChat streaming round-trip, Canvas command flow, browser-mode bootstrap.
- [ ] CI job runs Playwright headless against MSW mocks (and optionally a real `weft gateway`).
- [ ] Failures emit screenshots + traces.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh` exposes the e2e suite (e.g. `scripts/build.sh ui-e2e`).

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

MSW already covers all 9 endpoint groups; the Playwright run can use MSW for fast deterministic CI.

name: ws09: ui — axe-core a11y audit and bundle-size budget
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #24 + Deferred Phases S3.5.

## Problem / gap

No axe-core / WCAG AA audit run; no `vite-bundle-analyzer` in CI. Current bundle is 452 KB JS / 128 KB gzip; S1.2 exit criteria targeted < 200 KB gzip — currently over budget.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] axe-core integrated into the Playwright suite or run as a standalone script across all 14 routes.
- [ ] WCAG AA violations triaged: critical fixed, others tracked.
- [ ] `vite-bundle-analyzer` produces a report in CI.
- [ ] Bundle size budget set; build fails if gzip JS exceeds budget.
- [ ] Identified large modules code-split (`/canvas` advanced renderers, charting, code editor) via dynamic import.

## Dependencies

- Playwright suite item (axe runs nicest inside Playwright).

## Notes

The largest wins are likely in `/canvas` advanced renderers and the code-editor view.

name: ws09: auth — Tailscale provider and per-user session isolation
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #25 + Deferred Phases S3.5.

## Problem / gap

The gateway only supports the local Bearer-token flow today. SPARC plan calls for a Tailscale auth provider validating `X-Tailscale-User-*` headers plus source-IP / cert verification, and per-user session isolation so memory / sessions / config scopes do not leak across users.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Auth provider abstraction with `Bearer` (existing) and `Tailscale` implementations.
- [ ] Tailscale provider validates `X-Tailscale-User-Login`, source-IP within tailnet, and (optionally) cert chain.
- [ ] Per-user scoping for `/api/sessions`, `/api/memory`, `/api/config` so user A cannot see user B's data.
- [ ] Provider selection driven by config; default remains Bearer for local-dev.
- [ ] Integration tests cover an unauthorized request and a cross-user scope leak attempt.

## Dependencies

- `use-auth` hook work for the client-side scope token plumbing.

## Notes

Document the threat model in a new ADR (see ADR-for-BackendAdapter task) or its own ADR.

name: ws09: deploy — multi-stage Dockerfile for dashboard
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #26 + Deferred Phases S3.5 (S1.2.12 deployment artefact).

## Problem / gap

`clawft-ui/Dockerfile` is missing. Plan-of-record specifies a multi-stage Vite build -> `nginx:alpine` runtime, never written.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `clawft-ui/Dockerfile` with two stages: `node:lts-alpine` builder running `npm ci && npm run build`; `nginx:alpine` runtime serving `dist/`.
- [ ] Nginx config sets caching headers, MIME for `.wasm`, and SPA fallback to `index.html`.
- [ ] Image builds in CI; image size documented.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh` adds a `ui-docker` subcommand to drive it.
- [ ] `docs/ui/deployment.md` updated with the docker workflow.

## Dependencies

- Build script rename fix (so `clawft-ui/dist/` exists).

## Notes

Keep the runtime image minimal — no Node in the final image.

name: ws09: env — add .env and .env.mock with documented VITE vars
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #27 + Deferred Phases S3.5 (S1.2.13).

## Problem / gap

`clawft-ui/.env` and `.env.mock` are missing. New contributors have to read source to discover `VITE_API_URL`, `VITE_WS_URL`, `VITE_MOCK_API`, `VITE_BACKEND_MODE`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `clawft-ui/.env.example` (committed) and `clawft-ui/.env.mock` (committed) document all `VITE_*` vars consumed by the app with descriptions and sane defaults.
- [ ] `.gitignore` excludes `.env` (real local file) but includes `.env.example` and `.env.mock`.
- [ ] README points at `.env.example`.
- [ ] No secrets ever shipped in any committed env file.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Keep the var surface stable; downstream Tauri / Docker work will read the same env layer.

name: ws09: docs — author ADR for BackendAdapter contract
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #28 + ADR Coverage section.

## Problem / gap

The agent dashboard's auth model, BackendAdapter (axum/wasm/mock) indirection, MSW-first development pattern, and dual-mode runtime probe have no ADR. The audit explicitly calls this a documentation gap. Existing ADRs (003/005/006/007/013/015/016/038) target the WeftOS GUI / Explorer (workstream 08), not this dashboard.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New ADR under `docs/adr/` covering: `BackendAdapter` interface, mode selection (`?mode=` URL param, `VITE_BACKEND_MODE`, runtime health probe), MSW-first dev pattern, browser-only-mode encrypted IndexedDB key storage, and the auth model (Bearer + future Tailscale).
- [ ] Cross-references CHANGELOG 0.6.19 rename and the four step docs.
- [ ] Linked from `docs/ui/developer-guide.md`.
- [ ] ADR index updated.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Keep it short and decision-shaped, not a redo of the developer guide.

name: ws09: planning — refresh sparc trackers to reflect shipped status
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #29 + Orphaned/Stale Work.

## Problem / gap

`.planning/sparc/ui/05-ui-tracker.md` lists every row as `Status: Not Started`. `.planning/sparc/ui/01-phase-S1-foundation-core-views.md` lines 3053-3087 list every S1.x task as `Status: TODO`. Pre-implementation tasks P1-P6 (API design, WS protocol design, auth design, performance budget) are all `Not Started`. In reality the step docs and orchestrator log are the de-facto trackers and most of S1-S3.2 plus S3.6/S3.7 shipped.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `05-ui-tracker.md` rows updated to reflect observed status (Done / Partial / Deferred / Not Started) per the audit's status table.
- [ ] `01-phase-S1-foundation-core-views.md` rows reconciled with what landed.
- [ ] Pre-implementation P1-P6 either marked Done with a pointer to where the design landed (step docs) or explicitly marked deferred.
- [ ] No row remains `TODO` / `Not Started` for shipped work.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

Pure documentation hygiene; should not require code changes.

name: ws09: planning — note rename in step-7 phase-gate and re-run gate
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/09-clawft-agent-dashboard.md` task #30 + Open Questions #4.

## Problem / gap

`.planning/development_notes/step7-phase-gate.md` records the 11/11 PASS as run from `cd ui && npm run build` (1,920 modules). That command no longer exists post-rename. The doc is stale on the path; the gate has not been re-verified from `clawft-ui/`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `step7-phase-gate.md` annotated to note the 0.6.19 rename and the canonical path is now `clawft-ui/`.
- [ ] Re-run `scripts/build.sh gate` (after the build-script fix) and append the new metrics: modules transformed, bundle sizes, gzip sizes, time, workspace test count.
- [ ] If results regressed materially, file follow-up issues and link them.
- [ ] If results match the prior 11/11, doc records confirmation date.

## Dependencies

- `scripts/build.sh::cmd_ui` rename fix (so the gate can run end-to-end).

## Notes

Pair with the `.env.example` and README hygiene items so the gate run also exercises new dev-onboarding artefacts.
